2603.23292.title: LLM Olympiad: Why Model Evaluation Needs a Sealed Exam
2603.23292.paper_summary: Benchmarks and leaderboards are how NLP most often communicates progress, but in the LLM era they are increasingly easy to misread. Scores can reflect benchmark-chasing, hidden evaluation choices, or accidental exposure to test content -- not just broad capability. Closed benchmarks delay some of these issues, but reduce transparency and make it harder for the community to learn from results. We argue for a complementary practice: an Olympiad-style evaluation event where problems are sealed until evaluation, submissions are frozen in advance, and all entries run through one standardized harness. After scoring, the full task set and evaluation code are released so results can be reproduced and audited. This design aims to make strong performance harder to ``manufacture'' and easier to trust.
2603.23292.paper_summary_zh: 基準和排行榜是自然語言處理（NLP）最常用來傳達進展的方式，但在大型語言模型（LLM）時代，它們越來越容易被誤解。得分可能反映基準追逐、隱藏的評估選擇或意外接觸到測試內容——而不僅僅是廣泛的能力。封閉的基準延遲了這些問題，但降低了透明度，使社群更難從結果中學習。我們主張一種互補的做法：一種奧林匹克風格的評估活動，在評估之前問題是密封的，提交在提前凍結，所有參賽作品都通過一個標準化的框架運行。得分後，完整的任務集和評估代碼會被釋放，以便結果可以被重現和審計。這種設計旨在使強大的表現更難以「製造」，並更容易被信任。
2603.23292.author: Jan Christian Blaise Cruz et.al.
2603.23292.authors: Jan Christian Blaise Cruz, Alham Fikri Aji
